reference: These X-ray changes are due to hypertension, as long-standing hypertension causes hypertrophy of cardiac muscles, known as cardiomyopathy, which on X-ray shows cardiomegaly and prominent bronchovascular markings. Therefore, there is no need to take any other medication, but you should get your electrocardiogram and two-dimensional echocardiogram done for your cardiac assessment.
